findings.item: Hold a bounded playout jitter buffer and pace sends in real time
findings.cited: _clones/voice-ai/api/assistant-api/internal/channel/telephony/internal/telnyx/internal/audio_processor.go:118 as canonical for 'about 100 ms jitter buffer source target'
findings.issue: Line 118 is `func (p *AudioProcessor) bufferAndSendInput(audio []byte)` — an INPUT-side batching buffer that drains at `InputBufferThreshold = 32 * 60` (audio_processor.go:31) = 1920 bytes = 60 ms at 16 kHz linear16, not a 100 ms playout/jitter buffer. It is ingress batching, not the playout jitter buffer the item describes, and its value is 60 ms not 100 ms. The 100 ms figure is actually source-only (deepgram-ebook line 518 'a short jitter buffer around 100 milliseconds') and the 200 ms ceiling is LiveKit _output.py:45 `queue_size_ms=200` (both correctly cited). Note XPORT-06 does NOT cite this telnyx file (it cites livekit _output.py:45, pipecat server.py:306, transport.py:135, _pre_connect_audio.py). The telnyx code citation is a mismatched anchor.
findings.correct: Drop the telnyx audio_processor.go:118 anchor (60 ms ingress batch, not a 100 ms playout jitter buffer); the 100 ms target is source-only via deepgram-ebook:518 and the 200 ms ceiling is the only OSS playout-queue clone (LiveKit _output.py:45). Mark the 100 ms as source-only/unverified-in-clone for the playout-buffer mechanism.

summary: 9 of 10 Audio Transport items verified clean. frames.py:855-856 (16000/24000), deepgram stt.py:568-573 (encoding/sample_rate kwargs) and :652-663 (10 s idle / 5 s keepalive / 3-5 s recommended), soxr CLEAR_STREAM_AFTER_SECS=0.2, base_transport.py:69 (audio_out_10ms_chunks=4) and :72-73 (2 s end silence), base_output.py:82-135 (10 ms chunking), twilio.py:149-151 (clear/streamSid) / :251-258 (ulaw) / :267-274 (DTMF), LiveKit _output.py:45 (queue_size_ms=200), webrtc types.go:11-16 (Opus 48 kHz/20 ms/useinbandfec=1) and streamer.go:168-178, basic_dtmf_agent.py exists, and the aiortc-FEC 'unverified' flag is honest (no useinbandfec in clones outside voice-ai/webrtc). Only issue: item 6 cites a telnyx input-batching buffer (60 ms) as the canonical line for a '~100 ms playout jitter buffer', a mismatched anchor; the number itself is correctly source-grounded elsewhere, so MINOR.
